raw_consumer_statement: On Monday, XX/XX/year>, I received an alert on my cellphone that my Citibank XXXX XXXX XXXX had been charged {$4200.00} to XXXX XXXX. I did not recognize this charge, or the merchant and I immediately called Citibank to report the fraudulent charge and to cancel the card. Citibank canceled my card, issued a new card, flagged the charge as under dispute, and credited the charge shortly afterwards. Seven days later, on XX/XX/year>, the exact same charge to the same vendor came through a second time. Over the next several weeks I called Citibank a total of 13 times to report this second fraudulent charge and Citibank has done nothing to resolve the issue. Ive been transferred around to multiple departments including the XXXX department and the XXXX department. Ive spoken to several supervisors who force me to repeat the same information over and over again. They act confused about how this could have happened and then they promise to report the issue to the appropriate department. As of Wednesday, XX/XX/year>, nothing has happened and the second fraudulent charge is still on my account. There is nothing to indicate that the charge is even being disputed. Citibank screwed up and allowed a fraudulent charge to come through a second time on a canceled card and they refuse to do anything to fix their mistake. My due date is XX/XX/year> and Im going to be forced to make a payment and pay interest on a fraudulent charge that they allowed to happen.
